item.title: Re: Milestones; or the work formerly called "History Itself"
item.content:encoded:
Updating sources:

Source of SCP-4000-1 comes from here

Source of SCP-4000-2 comes from here

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Everyone else said that this entry is worthy to be 4000, but I myself think that this… this could even stand among 001 proposals. The beginning and the end of things, it all started and ended with words, language.

I'd +2 if I could, this is one of my favourite entry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this, though it could have been shorter and I still barely understand half of it.

+ My guess
- hide

SCP-4179 is a primordial language, possibly predating humanity itself, that can only be transmitted verbally. SCP-4179-1 is Mnemosyne, the goddess of memory, but her daughter Clio, muse of history, usurped her and made her lose (some of) her powers and sanity. SCP-4179-1 can make magic rocks, SCP-4179-2, which contain memories significant to where the original rock was from. Making one out of a piece of her own house gave her back her memories, and she's pissed. The last bit is about the Foundation capturing Clio.

It's very confusing what the actual SCP is, and nowhere does it actually directly say what SCP-4179-2 are (it explains what SCP-██-███-2 are, but you'd think personnel reading this would need to know that they're the same thing). The main SCP, the language, almost seems to be a vehicle to tell the story of Clio and SCP-4179-1.

I don't know if "Daevan" is an accepted synonym for Daevite.

You might want to add a footnote explaining what anxiolytic means (also I think it's misspelled)

9x9x7.5 slab of seafloor sedimentary rock.

You're missing units here.

SCP-4179-1 demanded to speak to Dr. Calibax, who agreed to do so on its environment to facilitate re-containment.

Does "on its environment" mean something like "on its territory" or did you mean something else?

I found it kind of annoying that the footnotes were all hidden in the last collapsible. Was that intentional?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for noting this! I'll see about reaching out to staff about it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The appropriate tags are missing aside from the '4000' one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree, this really needs to be 4000 especially with the continuity of 1000 2000 3000 etc. mentioned within the article itself. It's a shame it didn't get chosen.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've read a lot of 5 series SPC that were far better 4000 than actuall 4000 and this is one of them.
I believe 4000, just like 1000 2000 and 3000 should be a kind of new addition to baseline cannon that all other hubs should follow. Although the ending is not the best for that kind of thing, as it is too heavily pointing towards allowing all anomalies to end. If you'd polish out some things, a few misspellings, general chaos with numbers and make early parts of this huge SCP wall of text easier to read it would definitelly be one of the top SCP there are.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love the article, but the pictures seem to be broke

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I loved everything before the dialogue, as the other commenter did. I had run out of attention span by the last few stone entries, the ones that didn’t describe events, and I don’t feel like they added a lot and I just glossed over them.

This timeline idea is so dramatic so drastic that I feel like you either love it or hate it. I hate it. The goddess thing was cool, interview length would have been boring but bearable if I liked the topic more. Even more than the timeline idea I hated the idea of words having had that much power in scp verse (obviously authors’ words in our universe have power in scp verse). I also hate pataphysics and other stuff like that so I am going to down vote this.
-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As I mentioned in PMs, this could use some trimming, but I enjoyed it. I'm especially pleased with 1000's use, since a central aspect of 1000 for me is that they dealt with and/or used numerous temporal anomalies before their civilization's end — even though that's not a central aspect of the actual SCP article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1 for the sheer number of moments I enjoyed out of this, although you had me swinging back and forth on it a lot.

Regarding things that could be cut, for the big one, there were way too many -2 descriptions and/or too much detail, to the point that (on mobile) I was getting angry just seeing the next one. Seeing D-Class get maimed added next-to-nothing to this, and I never read the stone shape descriptions at all. If there was some special logic for including all the SCP stones you did, it wasn't worth it.

And then so many reacted/contentless ones? No.

Besides that… I guess the mother's speech.was very ostentatious and slightly repetitive, so that part could be trimmed. Clio could probably be shortened up. Maybe in the language about the timelines, or her and her mother's past. Again, maybe look for repetition between statements or extra flourish that doesn't add much? Sorry I don't have as much direct advice here, unlike the -2s.

Edit: specifically look for where you've told the same story or parts of the same story twice? Without looking back, I feel like that happens a lot. Edit edit: Or times when a character is protesting something repeatedly.

SCP-2000 being an ancient vault thing was something I didn't like. That's not a cutting suggestion, just registering my opinion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Honestly, I was enjoying it up until the interview.

The language, SCP-4000-1, and the SCP-4000-2 instances have wonderful logic to them that really make that part of the article a blast read, and you are rather creative in the kinds of memories and effects the various ripe stones impart. Had you settled there with a kicker entry on the log (kind of like in Tatiana's Prison) I would have upvoted in a heartbeat.

But then we get to the interview, and the piece bogs down in such a massive storm of all caps and exposition that the pacing stops and, at least for me, the reader is left to slog through the rest.

I can appreciate the ambition of this piece, and the theme of the impact of the various X000 articles on the wiki. However, as rumetzen aptly said above, this could have probably gotten the same impact in about half the length.

No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I noticed some of those mistakes. Glad someone else brought them up.

This is just an idea: instead of "peninsular" Spain, try "Iberian Spain".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Glad you liked it, Salt; I just hope Series V includes a bit of everything, since I couldn't even hope to write like this every time… but long-winded articles are kind of my thing. ^^U

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Glad you liked it, Ranavalona! I'll try to write more of it, and everybody is welcome to write about it too. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks, philocke! That's very generous of you! :D

If you do re-read, send me that list. It'd be great. Also, there are other items that would work better as SCP-4000 and are fairly easier and more engaging to read, but the vertebrating quality this one has was not meant to be its winning quality. I just loved 1000, 2000 and 3000 and wanted to make a heartfelt tribute, then my own thing.

Thank you again, and see you around!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I understand. I'm very sorry for taking so much of your time (and I swear I mean that sincerely, since unnecessary length is an issue I just can't seem to shake). I'd love some specific pointers on what to remove or re-work for after the contest, but I do understand just reading it is a bit too much (since there is just too much of the damn thing). Thank you for your feedback, rumetzen.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is, as I mention in another comment, an issue with my writing that I struggle with in everything I write. I'll try to improve; if not on re-write, on some other article. Thank you for your feedback nonetheless, seagull!

item.title: Thanks!
item.content:encoded:
++Thanks for your review, TL333s!
– hide block

Thank you for your words and notes! This comment was very useful to me, I'll correct the mistakes you kindly pointed out as soon as I can give the whole thing a read-over.

I think you could do something else to better show them switching languages.

I was tired to put so many notes in the dialogs. (Or, rather, he suspected he was tiring the readers. Then again, if people aren't tired of him by the time they get to the dialogs…)

It's just a huge lore dump with nothing to hang it on.

Well, that wasn't my goal at all and I am really sorry about it. I mean it sincerely; I'd love some specific pointers at some point later on, after the contest is over, to either re-write this or at least learn from my mistakes.

"Ripe" SCP-4000-2 cases may only be handled by D-Class subjects while placed inside Black Box facilities to prevent or mitigate potentially contagious memetic, cognitive or info-hazardous breakouts. SCP-4000-2 cases not contained by MTF Alpha-1 Coherence Units dispatched to retrieve them will be handled as Safe items and stored in Standard Secure Lockers, as their dimensions require.

Since SCP-4000-2 items can contain sensitive information, including Foundation-wide used memes and anti-memes that people may not be cleared for, only D-Class subjects inside facilities designed to recognize them and obscure them (the Black Box, albeit I just resigned to not explaining anything about them since this articled had become unwieldy enough by that time), amnesticizing them right after researchers are done with them, may handle the things. MTF Alpha-1 sends specially cleared personnel to collect them. The rest are Safe and store in SSLs, since they are, well, Safe by Box Test standards.

Boy, that's an awful lot of addenda that I just have no idea what the purpose of was. c.c

You don't wanna know how much didn't make it to this version. I know it is an issue with this skip… with most of what I write, really. Again, I'm very sorry about this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This. This is what the 4000 needs to be for me. This is the direction I want the site to take in Series V, absolutely huge themes depicted in a lighter way. You took all the previous X000s, put them together, had History Herself cracking jokes before being subdued by an MTF and made it work.
Is this long? Yes. Is the pace a bit slow? Yes. But this just reels you in before the last interview.
My most plus-oned +1 in the entire contest.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i would love to see manifestations of the other muses in other articles. the world built here is pretty cool.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is about twice as long as it actually needs to be.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a bit long for me. I really wanted to upvote after the beginning, but I felt like I was skimming so much content that I was missing out on the entire skip. The ending is good, but I felt disengaged for most of the stuff in between.

Novote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1 The only submission so far that I've thought while reading, "This needs to be 4000". I love the far reaching implications of this for the foundation and all of history, and tying in a bit of meta with the other X000's for good measure.

There are a few typos throughout, if I re-read I'll make a list for you, though they didn't detract from the story. Very well written.

The pace was a bit slow to start, drawing the reader in more and more. Good for a tale, maybe a bit slow for an SCP, and it may be off-putting for some. The meat of the SCP isn't until that last interview. With the story you've written I don't think it should be any different though, it flows very well.

Edit: Also, I hope you don't mind, I linked to your entry on the SCP subreddit. I'd really like this one to get some more attention. :) lol

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ This is totally my jam.
- And this post is probably too long

I get the sense that emotional communication is near impossible in SCP-4000 because you're actually conveying the precise way you feel, which is inherently not understandable by another person.

"Ripe" SCP-4000-2 cases may only be handled by D-Class subjects while placed inside Black Box facilities to prevent or mitigate potentially contagious memetic, cognitive or info-hazardous breakouts. SCP-4000-2 cases not contained by MTF Alpha-1 Coherence Units dispatched to retrieve them will be handled as Safe items and stored in Standard Secure Lockers, as their dimensions require.

I'm not sure if I mean this as a criticism, but hardly any of this makes sense.

The watermark on -1's file is obscuring the text. Also, I'm not sure how I feel about the particulars of that file, though that would be more pertinent if she were the focal anomaly here.

Personnel instructed to not look at 2-51.

This should be -52.

Boy, that's an awful lot of addenda that I just have no idea what the purpose of was. c.c

Dr. Calibax continues to walk towards SCP-4000

That should be -1. There are a couple other instances of this.

Why is she talking in all caps? …Okay, that's supposed to be SCP-4000. I think you could do something else to better show them switching languages.

…I am reading this, and absorbing none of it. I have no idea what's going on. It's just a huge lore dump with nothing to hang it on.

With the continual reference to the other X000s, I feel like this could be a winner, but I'm mostly just bewildered.

Probably a hearty upvote after the contest.

item.title: Milestones; or the work formerly called "History Itself"
item.content:encoded:
I'm terrified proud to present the product of literal hundreds of hours of desperation inadequacy deep, deep obsession work: an SCP-4000 entry mostly composed of a single dialog between two original characters and crosslinking.

"Wow, Reach, you're just priming yourself for insta-failure there!"

WELL WHAT CAN I SAY I'M OPTIMISTIC.
i'm sorry I shouted

Thanks to the SCP-ES staff for putting up with my absences (particularly morhadow, whose patience and good wishes are worth far more than he may realize); you guys are the best. Also, big hugs for Metaphysician, whose contributions to the site give it a flavour that goes from sickly sweet to nightmare salty… and I can never have enough of it. <3

Thanks to the authors of SCP-1000, thedeadlymoose, SCP-2000, FortuneFavorsBold and SCP-3000, djkaktus; all-encompassing proposals that did not only rock, but set in stone our evolution as a community and the tone for many other great things still to come.

May we remember their wonderful concepts and exquisite executions for all time; theirs are contributions that made me grow, as they made this idea grow. This may be a grossly insufficient tribute, but I will be happy to just bring back a piece of what they inspired.

Now, also important:

I believe I obtained image SCP-4000-2] from here, a stock image site; I modified it afterwards with GIMP.

Image SCP-4000-2 comes from comes from this stock photo site.

Images errorinfo, MEMEBOT and SCP-4000 were made by me via a number of image manipulation programs. They kinda are posted under CC by default, I think?

Hope you enjoy it! As always, comments and criticisms will be appreciated. :3
